market.summary: US markets stabilizing on hopes for Middle East peace talks; Global art market maintains steady growth, with high-end demand resilient and cautious optimism following 2025 growth of 4% ($59.6 billion).
keyTrends: US markets responding to potential de-escalation in Middle East
Shift towards high-quality, rarity-driven supply; decrease in new talent representation at auctions
U.S. market retains dominance with 44% of global sales
Increased risk aversion: guarantee value share at NYC sales reached 78%

highlights: Market rebound driven by 9% growth in public auction sales
Impressionist and Modern art segments leading historical category recovery
Private sales remain critical as dealers adopt hybrid models
Geopolitical uncertainty continues to influence near-term volatility
